skill.name: optimize
skill.description: Improve interface performance across loading speed, rendering, animations, images, and bundle size. Makes experiences faster and smoother.
skill.summary: Guide frontend performance improvements with measurement, bottleneck analysis, and practical optimization tactics.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic import syntax, markdown backticks, weak-crypto keywords, and system reconnaissance terms. Review found these are documentation examples and performance guidance in SKILL.md, with no executable helper files, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection text. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from code-like examples that agents may adapt in user projects.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dynamic Import Example
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 65 shows a React lazy loading example using import(). It is documentation for code splitting and does not execute as part of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import() expression is inside a fenced JavaScript example. No executable script file or runtime path is present in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Classified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis classified markdown inline code and fenced code delimiters as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The affected lines are prose and examples for HTML, CSS, and JavaScript performance work.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited locations are markdown formatting or illustrative snippets. There is no Ruby file, shell command, command runner, or user-controlled command interpolation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The reported high-severity weak cryptography matches occur in frontmatter descriptions and a measurement warning. No cryptographic API or hashing algorithm is referenced.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural language about performance targets and real-device measurement. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic primitive appears in context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Performance Inspection Terms
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance matches refer to layout, animation, and browser profiling guidance. The skill does not collect host information or instruct unauthorized environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss frontend rendering behavior and common performance mistakes. No evidence found of host reconnaissance commands, credential discovery, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Slow interfaces lose users and make polished products feel unreliable. This skill guides measured frontend improvements across loading, rendering, animations, images, and bundles.
actual_capabilities: Identifies frontend bottlenecks across loading, rendering, network, and runtime behavior.
Recommends image, font, CSS, JavaScript, and bundle size optimizations.
Guides Core Web Vitals improvements for LCP, INP, FID, and CLS.
Suggests React and framework-neutral rendering improvements.
Defines verification steps using before-and-after performance measurements.
Highlights common mistakes that make performance work ineffective.
limitations: It does not run Lighthouse, WebPageTest, or browser profiling automatically.
It needs project files or measured performance data for precise recommendations.
It provides guidance, not guaranteed performance gains for every application.
It does not replace real-device testing on slow networks.

use_cases.title: Improve a Slow Product Page
use_cases.description: Find high-impact fixes for images, fonts, scripts, and layout shifts on a customer-facing page.

use_cases.title: Plan a Web Vitals Sprint
use_cases.description: Turn Core Web Vitals problems into a focused list of measurable engineering tasks.

use_cases.title: Review an Animation-Heavy Interface
use_cases.description: Check whether animations use efficient properties and avoid expensive layout or paint work.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Performance Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for one page or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the optimize skill to review this page for obvious loading, image, CSS, and JavaScript performance issues. Prioritize fixes by user impact.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Core Web Vitals
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused improvement after receiving metric data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the optimize skill to analyze these Core Web Vitals results. Explain likely causes for poor LCP, INP, FID, or CLS and suggest measurable fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Runtime Jank
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for slow interactions and animations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the optimize skill to inspect this interaction for rendering, layout thrashing, long tasks, and animation bottlenecks. Recommend safe changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Performance Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across a product area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the optimize skill to build a phased performance plan for this feature. Include measurement steps, expected impact, implementation risk, and verification criteria.

output_examples.input: A landing page has a slow hero image and poor Largest Contentful Paint.
output: Prioritize the hero image because it controls the perceived first load.
Serve correctly sized modern image formats and preload the primary asset.
Measure LCP before and after the change on mobile and slow connections.

output_examples.input: A dashboard becomes choppy while scrolling a long list.
output: Check for layout reads mixed with style writes during scroll.
Virtualize the list and debounce expensive handlers.
Verify frame timing in browser performance tools after each change.

output_examples.input: A React route loads a large chart library on first paint.
output: Split the chart into a lazy-loaded component when it is not immediately visible.
Confirm the bundle analyzer shows a smaller initial JavaScript payload.
Test that loading states and accessibility behavior remain correct.

best_practices: Measure current performance before changing code, then verify the result with the same metric.
Prioritize changes that improve user-perceived speed on real devices and slow networks.
Keep accessibility and product correctness intact while optimizing performance.
anti_patterns: Optimizing small code paths while ignoring large images, scripts, or layout shifts.
Lazy loading above-the-fold content that users need immediately.
Adding will-change broadly without measuring memory and compositing costs.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides analysis and remediation, but you must provide measurements or run tools separately.

faq.question: Can it help with Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for LCP, FID, INP, and CLS improvement work.

faq.question: Is it only for React projects?
faq.answer: No. It includes React-specific tips, but most guidance applies to modern frontend applications.

faq.question: What inputs make it most useful?
faq.answer: Provide affected pages, performance traces, Lighthouse results, bundle reports, or symptoms users notice.

faq.question: Will it preserve accessibility?
faq.answer: The guidance explicitly warns against sacrificing accessibility while improving speed.

faq.question: Can non-engineers use it?
faq.answer: Yes. Product and design teams can use it to understand performance problems and request focused engineering work.
